channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: As women seek open congressional seats in Maryland, reproductive rights are front and center
item.description: In an election where the future of reproductive rights is on the ballot in Maryland and elsewhere across the country, the state’s all-male congressional delegation stands to gain an influx of women.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance says 'no' when asked whether Trump lost 2020 election
item.description: In the months since he became Donald Trump’s running mate, Ohio Sen. JD Vance has repeatedly deflected questions about whether the Republican presidential nominee lost the 2020 election, saying he was focused on the future.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris' interview with Fox News is marked by testy exchanges over immigration and more
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris engaged in a combative interview with Fox News on Wednesday, sparring with anchor Bret Baier on immigration and shifting policy positions while asserting that if elected, she would not represent a continuation of Joe Biden's presidency.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lorena Biasotti, CCSD school board candidate, discusses priorities ahead of election
item.description: Lorena Biasotti, a mother of four and candidate for Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, spoke about her platform on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris, Bret Baier spar over immigration at outset of Fox News interview
item.description: Vice President Harris on Wednesday sparred at length with Fox News anchor Bret Baier over immigration in a rare sit-down with the network. Baier opened the interview by asking Harris how many immigrants had crossed the southern border during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk commits $70 million to boost Donald Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, a tech mogul who is the world's richest person, plunged more than $70 million into helping Donald Trump and other Republicans win in November's election, making him one of the biggest donors to GOP causes this campaign season, according to campaign finance disclosures re
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Striking Boeing workers boo after Democratic Sen. Maria Cantwell criticizes Trump
item.description: Sen. Maria Cantwell, D-Wash., was booed after mentioning a housing shortage and blaming it on former President Trump during a union rally with striking Boeing workers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: The Nebraska Supreme Court rejected the contention that the state’s pardon board has sole authority to restore such rights.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Harris touts oil production during 2024 run after saying companies need to 'pay the price' for climate change
item.description: Vice President Harris previously said that oil companies should be investigated for climate change and pollution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter, at age 100, casts his 2024 ballot by mail
item.description: His son Chip Carter said before the family gathering that his father had this election very much in mind.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FEMA leader: Misinformation about hurricane operations must stop
item.description: "The ongoing spread of misinformation about operations is not OK," FEMA Administrator Deanne Criswell said.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Judge in Trump election case directs prosecutors to turn over info related to Pence documents probe
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judge overseeing the election interference case against Donald Trump directed prosecutors Wednesday to search for and provide to the former president's lawyers any Justice Department information related to a separate investigation into Mike Pence's handling of classifie
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris campaign hosts separated families ahead of Trump's Univision town hall
item.description: The Harris campaign has mostly centered its immigration and border security messaging on enforcement, drawing some criticism from advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Rufus Wainwright blasts Trump playing 'Hallelujah' at town hall
item.description: The 51-year-old singer said Tuesday that he was "horrified" to learn his 2001 cover of Leonard Cohen's "Hallelujah" was part of the playlist at the Trump campaign event a night earlier.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Voting rights groups seek investigation into Wisconsin text message
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Voting rights advocates on Tuesday asked state and federal authorities to investigate anonymous text messages apparently targeting young Wisconsin voters, warning them not to vote in a state where they are ineligible.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump clashes with Bloomberg editor over tariffs
item.description: “That is going to have a serious effect on the overall economy,” Bloomberg’s top editor, John Micklethwait said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Geraldo Rivera backing Harris: Trump 'cannot be trusted to honor the Constitution'
item.description: Television personality Geraldo Rivera revealed Monday that he is backing Vice President Kamala Harris over former President Donald Trump in the election, now less than a month away.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Walz to unveil Harris' plan for rural voters as campaign looks to cut into Trump's edge
item.description: Democratic vice presidential nominee Tim Walz will unveil his ticket’s plans to improve the lives of rural voters as Vice President Kamala Harris looks to cut into former President Donald Trump’s support.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: In divided St. Cloud, challengers hope to flip House seats
item.description: While many legislative districts in Minnesota are either solidly Republican or DFL, in the St. Cloud, voters are evenly split. Races for the two House seats that represent the region are among the most competitive in the state.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Harris brings a bit of Trump into her rally to try to show him as ‘unhinged’
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris wants voters to pay more attention when Trump talks about “the enemy within.” On Monday, she played a highlight reel to paint him as “increasingly unstable and unhinged.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram and Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Marie Gluesenkamp Perez, challenger Joe Kent face off in Washington 3rd District debate
item.description: One of the races that could determine the fate of Congress this election season happens to be the rematch race for Washington's 3rd District, and on Monday night, the candidates will debate on KOIN 6.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, NewsNation

item.title: Giant pandas will arrive at D.C.'s National Zoo this week
item.description: After an 11-month wait, giant pandas will populate the zoo once again. A pair of pandas, which left China on FedEx's "Panda Express" cargo jet, are expected to arrive in the D.C. area on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx, MPR News

item.title: Election Day Looms: Hurricane impacts on voting
item.description: Voting rules changed for impacted Florida counties after hurricane Helene, now the question is, will rules be adjusted after hurricane Milton?
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: One-on-one with Mississippi's candidates in 2024 US Senate race
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (WJTV) - U.S. Senator Roger Wicker (R-Miss.) and his Democratic opponent, Ty Pinkins, spoke to WJTV 12 News ahead of the November General Election. Wicker, a Pontotoc native, has been serving Mississippi in the U.S. Senate since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wright, NewsNation

item.title: With hunting party, Walz invites moderate male voters into the fold
item.description: Independent voter David Clayton joined Gov. Tim Walz at Saturday’s pheasant opener and says he’s sold on voting for the Harris-Walz ticket. He thinks others may be, too, but aren’t talking about it. “There are many, many MAGA-minded Americans who aren’t really so MAGA-minded.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Megan Burks, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: 3 voter-related lawsuits dropped in Nevada ahead of Election Day
item.description: Petitioners who filed three lawsuits challenging the maintenance of Nevada’s voter rolls have dismissed their cases, in part because voting by-mail ballot was already well underway.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eric Adams is 'working well through the chaos': Gov. Hochul
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul offered support for embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams during the Columbus Day Parade in Manhattan on Monday. "Told him to bring in new blood… he's doing that… working well through the chaos," she told PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bob Woodward takes NPR behind the headline-grabbing moments in his new book
item.description: Bob Woodward speaks to NPR about the revelations in his new book, and recounts how key moments and meetings in recent years played out behind closed doors.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow, Courtney Dorning, and Avery Keatley, MPR News

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson discusses government transparency, public safety, and housing
item.description: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Friday to discuss issues she believes need to be addressed in the city.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News
